pageTitle: Interval Timer - Free Workout Set & Rest Timer | GetNiftyKit
metaDescription: Enter your number of sets, work time, and rest time, and this interval timer runs the whole session for you — with sound and visual alerts at every transition.
softwareAppDescription: A free workout timer that automatically runs a full session based on your sets and work/rest durations, alerting you with sound at every phase change.
subtitle: Just enter your sets and work/rest times, and it runs the whole session for you
taglineExtra: Get sound and visual alerts at every phase change, accurate even when the tab is in the background.
invalidInputError: Please enter a number of sets and work/rest times of 1 or more.
infoIntroP: Interval Timer is a tool that automatically calculates and runs the full timeline of a workout session based on the number of sets and your work and rest durations. It alerts you with sound and a color change every time the phase switches between work and rest, so you don't have to keep watching the screen. Timing is calculated from real timestamps, so it stays accurate even if your screen turns off or you switch to another tab.
infoHowtoP: Enter your number of sets, work time, and rest time (in seconds), then press "Start." When a work phase ends, it automatically switches to rest, then back to work for the next set, repeating all the way through the last set. You can pause at any time and pick up right where you left off with "Resume," or press "Reset" to set everything up again from scratch.
infoUseCases: Keeping exact rest times between sets during weight training
Running interval workouts like circuit training or Tabata
Following along by sound during home workouts without staring at your phone
Reproducing a specific set/rest structure from a PT or group class

faq.q: Is it still accurate if I turn off my screen or switch tabs?
faq.a: Yes. The timer calculates elapsed time from real timestamps, so even if the browser is in the background and screen updates slow down, the actual phase transitions stay accurate.
There's no audio file — the alert tones are generated on the fly using the browser's Web Audio API, so there are no licensing concerns and it stays lightweight.

faq.q: Does the session include a rest period after the last set?
faq.a: No. The session ends as soon as the final set's work phase is complete — rest periods only occur between sets.

privacyNote: This tool runs the timer entirely in your browser. Your settings are never sent to or stored on a server.
